properties.fullDescription: A beautifully updated Georgian period town house built circa 1746 convenient for the town centre and nearby country & river walks. The property has been sympathetically renovated to provide a stylish home with accommodation comprising: entrance hall, kitchen with fitted units & marble work surfaces, lounge with open fireplace, hall, utility and shower room to ground floor. Three bedrooms and bathroom with separate shower to first floor. Outside the property has a private enclosed low maintenance walled garden. ACCOMMODATION Front entrance door with fanlight above through to the: ENTRANCE HALL Having coved ceiling, dado rail, laminate flooring, wood panelling to one wall with coat hooks and shoe storage under. KITCHEN 4.59m x 2.40m (15ft x 7ft 10in)Having sash window & part glazed stable style door to rear elevation, coved ceiling and laminate flooring. Fitted with a range of base & wall units with marble effect work surfaces & splashbacks comprising: belfast style sink with mixer tap & ceramic hob inset to work surface, cupboards, drawers, integrated dishwasher and integrated electric oven under, cupboards & concealed cooker hood over. Tall unit to side housing integrated fridge & freezer. Work surface return with cupboard & integrated wine cooler under. LOUNGE 4.20m x 4.97m (13ft 9in x 16ft 3in)Having two sash windows with fitted shutters to front elevation, radiator, dado rail, laminate flooring and media wall featuring fireplace with cast iron insert, space for flat screen television over, cupboards to either side with LED lighting & shelving over. HALL Having coved ceiling, tile effect flooring, smoke alarm, staircase rising to first floor and understairs storage cupboard. Opening to the: UTILITY 1.92m x 1.56m (6ft 3in x 5ft 1in)Having inset ceiling spotlights, radiator, tile effect flooring, work surface with cupboard, space & plumbing for automatic washing machine under, larder style cupboard to side. SHOWER ROOM 1.96m x 1.82m (6ft 5in x 5ft 11in)Having sash window to rear elevation, inset ceiling spotlights, chrome heated towel rail, tile effect flooring and built-in cupboard housing gas fired boiler providing for both domestic hot water & heating. Fitted with a suite comprising: fully tiled shower enclosure with mixer shower fitting, close coupled WC and hand basin on stand with drawers & shelf under. FIRST FLOOR LANDING Having sash window to rear elevation, coved ceiling, smoke alarm and access to roof space. BEDROOM ONE 4.58m x 3.61m (15ft x 11ft 10in)Having two sash windows with fitted shutters to front elevation, coved ceiling, radiator, wall light points and feature William Morris wallpapered wall. BEDROOM TWO 3.58m x 3.57m (11ft 8in x 11ft 8in)Having sash window with fitted shutters to front elevation, coved ceiling, radiator, wall light points and feature William Morris wallpapered wall. BEDROOM THREE 2.42m x 2.61m (7ft 11in x 8ft 6in)Having sash window with fitted shutters to rear elevation, coved ceiling and radiator. BATHROOM 2.

properties.title: 3 bedroom townhouse for sale
properties.fullDescription: A historic Grade II listed mixed use property situated in a prime town centre position, offering retail accommodation to the ground floor with flat above arranged over two floors. The ground floor comprises a well proportioned retail unit benefiting from prominent display frontage onto both the High Street and the Bull Ring , providing excellent visibility and strong pedestrian footfall. The upper floors provide a spacious flat arranged over two levels, offering flexible residential accommodation. The property also benefits from a small rear yard, brick built out buildings and useful pedestrian access to the Bull Ring. Offered with vacant possession, the property presents an ideal opportunity for investors seeking rental income or owner occupies looking to combine business and residential use. EPC rating E. Location The property is situated in the historic market town of Horncastle, located on the southern edge of the Lincolnshire Wolds, an area of outstanding natural beauty. Horncastle is a charming and well regarded town known for its traditional character, independent shops, antique centres, cafés and weekly markets .The town offers a good range of amenities including supermarkets, primary and secondary schooling, healthcare facilities, and a variety of leisure options as well as being the location for East Lindsey District Council's main office. Horncastle is conveniently positioned approximately 21 miles east of the city of Lincoln and 23 miles north west of Boston, with access to the wider region via the A158 connecting to the coastal resort of Skegness. The property's prime High Street and Bull Ring position places it at the heart of the town's commercial centre, benefiting from excellent visibility and foot fall. Retail Sales Area 3.73m x 7.48m (12ft 2in x 24ft 6in) With radiator & three display windows fronting onto the Bull Ring and High Street. Width measurement extending to 13' 5" (4.13 m) Secondary Retail Area 2.99m x 4.14m (9ft 9in x 13ft 6in) With two display windows, radiator. Rear Storeroom 3.85m x 3.94m (12ft 7in x 12ft 11in) With radiator & staircase to living accommodation.. Kitchen 2.32m x 2.85m (7ft 7in x 9ft 4in) With gas fired central heating boiler, stainless steel sink with worksurface and cupboard below, plumbing for washing machine and door opening to rear yard. Living Accommodation. Which is arranged over the first and second floor. Lounge 4.15m x 4.36m (13ft 7in x 14ft 3in) With radiator, dado rail and panelling below, sash window. Maximum measurements. Kitchen Diner 4.19m x 4.29m (13ft 8in x 14ft) With fitted wall and base cupboards, space for electric cooker, work surfaces, PVC sink , sash window, radiator and dado rail. Bathroom 2.61m x 3.66m (8ft 6in x 12ft)With roll top bath, close couple toilet, wash basin, radiator, part panelled walls, chrome heated towel rail, radiator and tiled flooring. Maximum depth measurement. Stairs To First Floor Landing With built in cupboard & access to roof space. Bedroom 1 2.83m x

properties.fullDescription: Character Property Requiring Renovation in the Heart of Horncastle – No Onward ChainOccupying a highly convenient position within the heart of the historic market town of Horncastle, Moss House presents an exciting opportunity to acquire a characterful period home offering generous accommodation, a wealth of potential and the chance to create a wonderful family residence tailored to individual tastes and requirements.Although requiring a programme of renovation and modernisation throughout, the property retains many attractive features and offers well-proportioned accommodation comprising a living room with feature fireplace, a separate dining room and kitchen to the ground floor.To the first floor are three double bedrooms together with a family bathroom, providing excellent space for families, investors or those seeking a restoration project in a desirable town centre location.Externally, the property enjoys a particularly attractive enclosed rear garden, offering a surprising degree of privacy and space. Predominantly laid to lawn with established borders, mature planting and a paved seating area, the garden provides an excellent outdoor environment for relaxation and entertaining. The garden is enclosed by attractive walling and benefits from gated access.The property is offered for sale with no onward chain, allowing for a straightforward purchase process.LocationHorncastle is a thriving and historic Lincolnshire market town, renowned for its character, independent shops, antique centres and strong sense of community. The town offers an excellent range of amenities including supermarkets, cafés, restaurants, public houses, healthcare facilities and well-regarded schooling, all within easy reach of the property.Often referred to as the "Gateway to the Lincolnshire Wolds", Horncastle is ideally positioned for those who enjoy outdoor pursuits, with the nearby Lincolnshire Wolds Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty offering miles of scenic walking, cycling and countryside exploration. The town also benefits from good road connections to Lincoln, Louth, Boston and the Lincolnshire coast.Arrange your viewing on the Purplebricks website or via the Purplebricks App.Property Ownership InformationTenureFreeholdCouncil Tax BandBProperty Description DisclaimerThis is a general description of the property only, and is not intended to constitute part of an offer or contract. properties.title: 4 bed property for sale
properties.description: a secluded and enviable plot within a modern development, Osborne Way is an impressive four-bedroom family home ...Listed on 1st Apr 2026
properties.fullDescription: Occupying a secluded and enviable plot within a modern development, Osborne Way is an impressive four-bedroom family home built in 2020 by the reputable Cyden Homes. This property perfectly balances the benefits of contemporary construction with a sense of privacy and space, all while offering striking views over the rolling Lincolnshire Wolds. The internal layout is generously proportioned across two floors, featuring three versatile reception rooms and two bathrooms designed to accommodate the demands of a growing family. Having one owner since new, the property has been upgraded continuously both internally and externally with the major addition of 14 solar panels and a storage battery system.The ground floor opens into a welcoming entrance hall with a convenient cloakroom and a dedicated study, providing a quiet space for those working from home. The formal lounge serves as a sophisticated retreat, centred around a bespoke media wall with an integrated electric flame-effect fireplace. At the heart of the home lies a stylish kitchen diner equipped with a range of integrated appliances, which flows seamlessly into a vibrant sunroom where patio doors lead out to the rear garden. A practical utility room, accessed directly from the kitchen, completes the ground-floor footprint.Upstairs, the galleried landing leads to four well-proportioned bedrooms. The master suite offers a touch of luxury with its own private en-suite shower room and fitted wardrobes, while the remaining bedrooms are served by a modern three-piece family bathroom. Externally, the property continues to excel with a thoughtfully designed two-tier garden. The lower "adults' area" features a paved terrace ideal for alfresco dining and socialising with provisions for a hot tub while the upper tier provides a safe, lawn area perfect for children's activities. To the front, a block-paved driveway provides ample parking and leads to a substantial double garage.Located in the historic market town of Horncastle, the property is ideally situated for family life. Parents will appreciate being within the catchment area for the prestigious Queen Elizabeth’s Grammar School and highly-regarded primary schools. The town centre offers a vibrant mix of independent boutiques, cafes, and essential amenities including supermarkets and a doctors' surgery. For outdoor enthusiasts, the nearby Lincolnshire Wolds, an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty, provides endless walking and cycling trails, while local parks and sports clubs offer plenty of weekend entertainment for all ages. The monthly repayments and interest rates provided are indicative and should be used as a guide only. The actual amount you can borrow and your final rate will depend on your personal financial situation and is subject to a full application. properties.fullDescription: Important note on imagery: Photos 3-9 have been professionally virtually refurbished SummaryThis substantial four-bedroom detached home offers a rare and exciting opportunity for buyers looking for a project with genuine scope to add value. Originally two semi-detached properties, the home has been partially converted into one dwelling, and now presents a flexible layout with plenty of potential for reconfiguration, modernisation and improvement ideal for investors, developers or buyers with vision.Important note: Photos 3,4,5,6,7,8 and 9 have been professionally virtually refurbished. The room measurements remain the same; the virtual refurbishment has been included to help you envision what could be achieved with the space.Internally, the property still retains features of its original configuration, including two staircases, two kitchens, and two bathrooms (one located on the ground floor and one on the first floor). With the right investment and imagination, this could be transformed into a fantastic family home, or potentially adapted to suit multi-generational living or other requirements (subject to any necessary consents).Externally, the property benefits from off-road parking, along with rear courtyard spaces positioned either side of the outbuildings offering useful outdoor areas that could be further enhanced. An added benefit of this property is it's excellent eco-friendly set up to help reduce day-to-day energy costs. It has a dedicated electric vehicle charger, it also features solar panels and the solar battery system that allows you to store generated electricity for later use.This is a home with a lot of potential and an opportunity to create something truly special. properties.fullDescription: Starkey&Brown are delighted to present for sale this modern, immaculately presented semi-detached home. Built in 2009 by the reputable Kier Homes, this property is in true turnkey condition, boasting spacious living accommodation across two floors and a beautifully landscaped garden. Upon entering, you are welcomed by an inviting entrance hall that leads to a convenient downstairs cloakroom/WC and a generous 16ft lounge. To the rear, the modern kitchen diner serves as the heart of the home, featuring French doors that open onto a sun-drenched, landscaped rear garden, perfect for alfresco dining. Upstairs, the property offers two comfortable double bedrooms, a further single bedroom, and a modern three-piece family bathroom which was stylishly updated just 18 months ago. There is also allocated parking with EV Car charging point and access to further parking. Situated in a popular residential area of Horncastle, the home is exceptionally well-located for a wealth of local amenities, including highly regarded primary and secondary schooling. To arrange your viewing, please contact Starkey&Brown. Council tax band: C. properties.description: An attractive detached bungalow occupying a generous larger-than-average plot in a sought-after position within the historic ...Listed on 14th May 2026
properties.fullDescription: An attractive detached bungalow occupying a generous larger-than-average plot in a sought-after position within the historic market town of Horncastle. Built by the highly regarded local developer Hugh Bourn Homes, the property offers well planned accommodation comprising: Entrance hall, kitchen, spacious lounge, two bedrooms and bathroom. Outside, the property benefits from a driveway providing ample off-road parking, a garage and extensive lawned gardens to the side and rear, offering excellent outdoor space and potential. Further benefits include gas central heating and double glazing throughout.EPC Rating: CLocationLiving in Horncastle offers a great balance of market town charm, countryside surroundings and everyday convenience, making it particularly appealing to families, retirees and those looking for a quieter pace of life.Some of the key advantages include:Historic market town atmosphere – Horncastle is well known for its characterful streets, independent shops, Georgian architecture and traditional market town feel. It has a friendly community and plenty of local character.Excellent access to the Lincolnshire Wolds – Situated on the edge of the Lincolnshire Wolds, an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty, the town is ideal for walking, cycling and enjoying the countryside.Popular antiques destination – Horncastle has built a reputation as one of Lincolnshire’s best antiques centres, attracting visitors from across the region with its antique shops, centres and regular fairs.Good local amenities – Despite its traditional feel, the town has supermarkets, cafés, pubs, restaurants, medical facilities, schools and sports clubs, making it practical for day-to-day living.Well placed for the coast and larger towns – The Lincolnshire coast, including Skegness and Mablethorpe, is within reasonable driving distance, while Lincoln is accessible for wider shopping, dining and rail connections.Strong sense of community – The town hosts regular events, markets and community activities throughout the year, helping create a welcoming atmosphere.Generally good value property market – Compared with many parts of the UK, Horncastle often offers larger homes and plots for better value, particularly attractive for buyers seeking more outdoor space.Education options – The town is home to well regarded schools, including Queen Elizabeth's Grammar School, which has a strong local reputation.Overall, Horncastle appeals to buyers looking for a combination of space, community, countryside access and traditional market town living, while still retaining good practical amenities.AccommodationPart glazed front entrance door through to the:Entrance HallHaving radiator, access to roof space and two built-in cupboards.Kitchen (3.26m x 2.31m)(10'8" x 7'7") Having window to front elevation, part glazed door to side elevation, radiator and tile effect vinyl flooring. properties.fullDescription: Situated on the ever-popular Malsters Drive development in Horncastle, this beautifully presented two bedroom home offers stylish, modern accommodation that is ready to move straight into. Finished to an exceptional standard throughout, the property has been meticulously maintained by the current owners, making it an ideal first-time purchase, investment or downsize.The accommodation begins with a welcoming entrance leading into a spacious and bright lounge, providing an excellent space to relax or entertain. To the rear of the property is a contemporary kitchen diner, fitted with a range of modern units along with an integrated dishwasher and offering ample space for a dining table. The enclosed rear garden provides a private outdoor space ideal for entertaining, relaxing or family enjoyment, with access available both through the French doors from the kitchen diner and via a shared side passage.Located within easy reach of Horncastle's excellent range of shops, cafés, schools and amenities, this immaculate home combines modern convenience with a highly sought-after location and is sure to appeal to a wide range of buyers. Early viewing is highly recommended to fully appreciate everything this superb property has to offer.Agent's Note: We have been informed by the owner that a maintenance charge will be payable in the future to cover the upkeep of the estate once it is fully completed. Ideally positioned on the edge of Horncastle, a thriving market town on the Lincolnshire Wolds. The property is within walking distance to a variety of local amenities, including independent shops, supermarket, pubs, and cafés.- 2 Bedroom Mid Terrace- Brand New Development- Available on Shared Ownership- Off Road Parking- Downstairs WC- Contact Us Today For More InformationLocal AreaThe county town of Lincoln is some 30 km west along the A158 where there is a wide range of high street and specialised shopping and public services, Horncastle is also located within easy driving distance of the glorious sandy beaches of East Lincolnshire coast and the Lincolnshire Wolds Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty.Families will appreciate the excellent local schooling, with the renowned Queen Elizabeth's Grammar School just a short stroll away, alongside a range of primary and secondary school options nearby.Transport & Road Links: Horncastle enjoys strong transport connections, with the A158 providing direct routes to Lincoln (approx. 21 miles west) and Skegness on the coast (approx. 20 miles east). properties.fullDescription: A spacious semi-detached family home, set on a generous plot within the popular market town of Horncastle.This well-proportioned property offers versatile and comfortable living accommodation throughout, ideal for family life. The ground floor comprises a welcoming entrance hall, a spacious living room, separate dining room, and a fitted kitchen, along with a large conservatory spanning the rear of the property-providing excellent additional living space and views over the garden. A useful utility room and downstairs W.C. Complete the ground floor.To the first floor, there are three well-sized bedrooms and a family bathroom, all accessed from a central landing.Externally, the property benefits from a block-paved driveway to the front, along with an additional gravelled parking area providing ample off-road parking, and a lawned front garden. There is also a detached single garage.To the rear, a generous and well-established garden offers a lawn, patio seating area, decorative borders, and a variety of mature shrubs and trees, along with a timber outdoor bar area-perfect for entertaining and enjoying the outdoor space.Further benefits include uPVC double glazing and air source heating pump, as well as the added advantage of solar panels, helping to reduce running costs and improve energy efficiency.Horncastle is a sought-after market town situated on the edge of the Lincolnshire Wolds, offering a range of shops, amenities, and a traditional market place.
